envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Role assessment composes against existing Wave 2.1 role-depth files; the Twin proposes a role match; the user confirms; the admin approves. Never workforce scoring.

items.name: Role Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Role Map. Confirms the user's actual role (vs title), maps against Wave 2.1 role-depth catalog, and proposes the Twin's day-one capabilities + first-week aha moments under admin governance.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks the user what they actually do; matches against the OOTB role-depth catalog; proposes Twin capabilities + first-week aha moments. Admin approves; Foundation governance authorizes any subsequent activation.
items.model_usage_notes: Title and actual role often diverge. Use both. Role-template match confidence must be reported transparently. Never workforce scoring.
required_questions: What is your actual role (what you do day to day)?
What role family describes the work (operational / executive / specialist / management)?
What seniority level (IC / senior IC / lead / manager / director / VP / C-level)?
What department do you operate inside?
Do you have direct reports? If so, how many and what kind?
What common decisions do you make?
optional_questions: What documents / artifacts do you produce?
What KPIs / metrics are you measured against (team-shaped, not employee-scoring)?
Who are your most frequent collaborators (role-shaped)?
Which first-week aha moments would be most valuable for you?
How well does the OOTB role template at docs/ootb-catalog/role-depth/ match your actual role?
admin_questions: Which roles require additional governance review (executive / regulated / legal / HR / finance)?
Which OOTB role templates apply verbatim and which require company-specific overrides?
user_questions: Does the proposed Role Map match what you actually do?
What's missing or wrong in the proposed role map?
What sensitive scopes (personal / confidential / executive) must NOT be in the initial Map?
output_candidates: Role Map proposal (title + actual role + role family + seniority + department + manager + direct reports + responsibilities + decisions + collaborators)
Twin day-one capability candidates (admin approval required)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every Role Map activation
User must confirm their own role before activation
Sensitive scopes (personal / confidential / executive) excluded at intake remain absolute downstream
No workforce-scoring inferences from KPI questions
safe_defaults: Admin-approved Map region before any Twin capability lights up
OOTB role-depth defaults as starter priors
forbidden_defaults: Sensitive-attribute inference from role / responsibility data
DMW_scope_implications: Role Map bounds Role-scoped DMW memory per Wave 2.1 §25 per-role DMW scope notes
Sensitive scopes excluded at user-intake remain excluded at DMW activation
Role-template overrides constrain DMW memory categories
Twin day-one capabilities map to allowed DMW categories per OOTB role-depth files
